preamble: The transformation from a nasalized low vowel to a diphthong involves the development of a semi-vowel off-glide, often as a result of the loss of a following nasal consonant or as part of a merger in a language's phonological history.

examples.note: Historical development where Latin endings like -anum, -anem, and -onem merged into the nasal diphthong -ão (mapped here as 'au' for the diphthongal quality).

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
